original_hypothesis.title: VestingManager.setMarketplace allows admin to redirect marketplace authority, enabling arbitrary vesting transfers without updating MarketplaceSetting
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: VestingManager gates listVesting/unlistVesting/completePurchase/getVestingTokenAddress with onlyMarketplace. Admin can change marketplace address to any contract. If admin is compromised or malicious, they can point marketplace to an attacker-controlled contract that calls completePurchase/unlistVesting to move escrowed vestings from VestingManager to arbitrary buyers/sellers, bypassing the intended Marketplace listing checks and payment handling.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is not 'owner can do anything' generic: it is a specific privileged redirection that changes the sole gate protecting custody of pooled vestings held under address(this) in StepVesting. It represents a single-step custody takeover if admin trust is broken.

proof_of_signal.pattern: onlyMarketplace protects token-moving functions; admin can reassign marketplace to arbitrary address

preconditions: Attacker controls s2Admin (compromised key) or governance process
There exist escrowed vestings held by VestingManager in one or more StepVesting plans

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Redirectable sole-authority gate for custody-moving functions
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: A malicious marketplace can transfer vestings out without payment validation
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No timelock/2-step process; no cross-check with MarketplaceSetting.marketplace address

analysis_questions: Is there any on-chain linkage ensuring VestingManager.marketplace matches the deployed Marketplace proxy?
Does MarketplaceSetting store marketplace address and is it used anywhere for verification?
Could an attacker marketplace drain vestings by calling completePurchase with arbitrary buyer?
validation_requirements: Simulate: setMarketplace(attacker) then call completePurchase to transfer vestings to attacker
Assess whether StepVesting.transferVesting authorization permits VestingManager to move allocations (it does via manager role)
Review operational controls around s2Admin key

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Compromised admin sets VestingManager.marketplace to an attacker contract. Attacker contract calls completePurchase(attackerEOA, vestingPlan, amount) repeatedly, transferring pooled vestings from VestingManager to attacker without any payment token transfers, draining escrow.
prerequisites: Admin key compromise or malicious admin

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Theft of all escrowed vesting allocations held by VestingManager
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All sellers with active listings and buyers mid-flow relying on escrow
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Only via admin intervention/upgrade; allocations once transferred may be unrecoverable

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests asserting marketplace address immutability or governance constraints

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Monitor VestingManager.marketplace changes and require off-chain alerting

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
